 {"id":"bd-2ky9","title":"E2E Test Scripts with Comprehensive Logging","description":"# E2E Test Scripts with Comprehensive Logging

## Background
Plan Section Testing + Verification requires end-to-end tests that validate the complete pipeline from user input to terminal output. The user specifically requested "comprehensive unit tests and e2e test scripts with great, detailed logging."

## E2E Test Philosophy
E2E tests should:
1. Test the complete system as a user would experience it
2. Run in a PTY to capture real terminal behavior
3. Log every step for post-mortem debugging
4. Be deterministic and reproducible
5. Cover happy paths and error cases

## E2E Test Script Structure

### Directory Layout
```
tests/
  e2e/
    fixtures/          # Test input files
    expected/          # Expected output snapshots
    scripts/           # Test runner scripts
      run_all.sh       # Master test runner
      test_inline.sh   # Inline mode tests
      test_altscreen.sh # Alt-screen tests
      test_cleanup.sh  # Cleanup/panic tests
      test_input.sh    # Input handling tests
    lib/
      common.sh        # Shared utilities
      logging.sh       # Logging functions
      pty.sh           # PTY spawning helpers
```

### Logging Framework (logging.sh)
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Comprehensive logging for E2E tests

LOG_LEVEL=${LOG_LEVEL:-DEBUG}
LOG_FILE=${LOG_FILE:-/tmp/ftui_e2e_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log}

log() {
    local level=$1
    shift
    local timestamp=$(date +"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S.%3N")
    local caller="${BASH_SOURCE[2]}:${BASH_LINENO[1]}"
    echo "[$timestamp] [$level] [$caller] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

log_debug() { [[ "$LOG_LEVEL" == "DEBUG" ]] && log "DEBUG" "$@"; }
log_info()  { log "INFO" "$@"; }
log_warn()  { log "WARN" "$@"; }
log_error() { log "ERROR" "$@"; }

log_test_start() {
    local test_name=$1
    log_info "=========================================="
    log_info "STARTING TEST: $test_name"
    log_info "=========================================="
}

log_test_pass() {
    local test_name=$1
    log_info "✓ PASSED: $test_name"
}

log_test_fail() {
    local test_name=$1
    local reason=$2
    log_error "✗ FAILED: $test_name"
    log_error "  Reason: $reason"
    log_error "  Log file: $LOG_FILE"
}

log_hex_dump() {
    local label=$1
    local data=$2
    log_debug "$label (hex): $(echo -n "$data" | xxd -p | tr -d n)"
}
```

## E2E Test Categories

### 1. Inline Mode Tests (test_inline.sh)
Tests that streaming logs + UI chrome works correctly.

```bash
test_inline_log_scroll() {
    log_test_start "Inline mode: log scrolling"
    
    # Setup
    local pty_output=$(mktemp)
    log_debug "PTY output file: $pty_output"
    
    # Run ftui app that streams 100 log lines with 6-line UI
    log_info "Spawning ftui inline mode app..."
    timeout 10s script -q -c "./target/debug/ftui-harness --mode inline --ui-height 6 --log-count 100" "$pty_output"
    local exit_code=$?
    log_debug "Exit code: $exit_code"
    
    # Verify
    log_info "Verifying output..."
    
    # Check scrollback preservation
    if grep -q "Log line 1" "$pty_output"; then
        log_debug "Early log lines found in scrollback ✓"
    else
        log_test_fail "Inline log scroll" "Early log lines missing from scrollback"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check UI region intact
    if tail -6 "$pty_output" | grep -q "\[Status\]"; then
        log_debug "UI region present at bottom ✓"
    else
        log_test_fail "Inline log scroll" "UI region not at expected position"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_test_pass "Inline mode: log scrolling"
    rm "$pty_output"
}
```

### 2. Cleanup Tests (test_cleanup.sh)
Tests that terminal is restored after exit/panic.

```bash
test_panic_cleanup() {
    log_test_start "Panic cleanup: terminal restored"
    
    # Capture terminal state before
    local before_state=$(stty -g)
    log_debug "Terminal state before: $before_state"
    
    # Run app that panics
    log_info "Running app that will panic..."
    ./target/debug/ftui-panic-test 2>/dev/null || true
    
    # Capture terminal state after
    local after_state=$(stty -g)
    log_debug "Terminal state after: $after_state"
    
    # Verify restoration
    if [[ "$before_state" == "$after_state" ]]; then
        log_debug "Terminal state restored ✓"
    else
        log_test_fail "Panic cleanup" "Terminal state not restored"
        log_error "  Before: $before_state"
        log_error "  After:  $after_state"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check cursor visible (SGR sequence check)
    log_info "Checking cursor visibility..."
    # ... additional checks ...
    
    log_test_pass "Panic cleanup: terminal restored"
}
```

### 3. Input Tests (test_input.sh)
Tests for keyboard, mouse, paste handling.

### 4. ANSI Output Tests
Verify presenter output matches expected sequences.

## Test Output Format

Each test run produces:
1. Console output (PASS/FAIL per test)
2. Detailed log file with timestamps
3. JSON summary for CI
4. PTY captures for failed tests

### JSON Summary Format
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-01-31T23:45:00Z",
  "total": 50,
  "passed": 48,
  "failed": 2,
  "skipped": 0,
  "duration_ms": 12345,
  "tests": [
    {
      "name": "test_inline_log_scroll",
      "status": "passed",
      "duration_ms": 234,
      "log_lines": 45
    },
    {
      "name": "test_panic_cleanup",
      "status": "failed",
      "duration_ms": 567,
      "error": "Terminal state not restored",
      "log_file": "/tmp/ftui_e2e_20260131_234500.log"
    }
  ]
}
```

## E2E Test Inventory

| Test | Description | Priority |
|------|-------------|----------|
| inline_basic | Basic inline mode display | P0 |
| inline_log_scroll | Log scrolling preserves scrollback | P0 |
| inline_resize | Resize re-anchors UI | P0 |
| inline_cursor | Cursor position contract | P0 |
| altscreen_enter_exit | Alt-screen lifecycle | P0 |
| cleanup_normal | Terminal restored on exit | P0 |
| cleanup_panic | Terminal restored on panic | P0 |
| cleanup_io_error | Terminal restored on IO error | P1 |
| input_key_basic | Basic key parsing | P0 |
| input_key_modifiers | Modifier key detection | P1 |
| input_mouse_click | Mouse click handling | P1 |
| input_mouse_scroll | Mouse scroll handling | P1 |
| input_paste | Bracketed paste handling | P1 |
| input_focus | Focus events | P2 |
| ansi_sgr | SGR sequence output | P0 |
| ansi_osc8 | OSC 8 hyperlink output | P1 |
| ansi_sync | Synchronized output | P1 |
| unicode_basic | ASCII width handling | P0 |
| unicode_wide | CJK wide chars | P0 |
| unicode_emoji | Emoji rendering | P0 |
| unicode_zwj | ZWJ sequences | P1 |

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] run_all.sh executes all E2E tests
- [ ] Each test has detailed logging
- [ ] Failed tests produce actionable diagnostics
- [ ] JSON summary generated for CI
- [ ] PTY captures preserved on failure
- [ ] Tests run in < 60 seconds total
- [ ] All P0 tests must pass for v1

## Dependencies
- Requires: bd-2kj (Agent Harness exists to test)
- Requires: bd-10i.11.2 (PTY framework)
- Blocks: bd-2gx (Quality Gates)

## CI Integration
- Run on every PR
- Block merge on P0 test failures
- Upload log artifacts on failure","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T00:22:54.577032739Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T01:49:32.441537371Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T01:49:32.441432504Z","close_reason":"Implemented E2E scripts + harness env hooks; full run passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ky9","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ky9","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ky9","depends_on_id":"bd-2kj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":373,"issue_id":"bd-2ky9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"E2E suite: 31 tests, 6 suites, all passing in 39s.  {"id":"bd-2lus","title":"Demo Showcase: Input Macro Recorder + Scenario Runner","description":"## Background
A macro recorder turns the demo into a reproducible, scripted showcase. It should record input events, play them back deterministically, and support curated scenarios.

## Scope / Goals
- Record input events with timestamps
- Play back with normalized timing
- Scenario runner for guided demos
- UI to view timeline and replay markers

## Tests / Logging
- Unit tests for record/playback and timing normalization
- Snapshot tests for recorder UI states
- Determinism regression tests
- PTY E2E scripts with verbose logs and captured event traces

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Record + replay produces identical UI output
- [ ] Scenarios run without drift
- [ ] All tests pass

## Success Criteria
- Epic delivers a cohesive user-visible capability: Demo Showcase: Input Macro Recorder + Scenario Runner
- Child tasks are not just implemented; each has unit tests + E2E scripts with verbose JSONL logs and deterministic fixtures
- Graceful degradation tiers are specified (capabilities, fallbacks, safety) and validated in tests


## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.

 {"id":"bd-2lus.1","title":"Macro Recorder spec + UX flow","description":"## UX / Behavior Spec
- Controls: Record / Stop / Play / Loop / Speed
- Timeline view with markers (start/end, key events)
- Failure behavior: if playback fails, show error state + log
- Scenario runner: list of curated macros with descriptions

## Timing Model
- Normalize timing so playback is stable across machines
- Optional fixed‑step playback for deterministic tests

## Tests (Required)
- Unit tests for control state machine
- Snapshot tests for timeline states
- PTY E2E: record → stop → replay

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Clear control flow and error states
- [ ] Deterministic playback mode specified
- [ ] Test matrix included

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.

 {"id":"bd-2lus.2","title":"Macro Recorder implementation","description":"## Implementation Outline
- Recorder engine: capture Event stream with timestamps
- Playback engine: schedule events with normalized timing
- UI: timeline + controls + status
- Scenario runner: list of macros with metadata

## Tests
- Unit: record/playback order
- Determinism regression (bd-2lus.4)
- Snapshot tests for UI (bd-2lus.3)
- PTY E2E (bd-2lus.7)

## Logging
- Debug log each event during record/playback when enabled

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Record + replay matches expected sequence
- [ ] Playback speed controls work
- [ ] No panics under rapid input

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.

 {"id":"bd-hsvho","title":"Diagram Type: Pie Chart","description":"Background

- Parse pie syntax (title + labeled values, showData if supported).

Considerations
- Avoid label collisions; use fallback legend if needed.
- Deterministic ordering of slices.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for slice ordering and label placement.
- Snapshot tests for varied slice counts.
- PTY E2E logs: diagram_type=pie, size, tier, warnings.

Success Criteria
- Pie charts render clearly at multiple sizes.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:40:20.737661921Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T14:45:52.173183529Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T14:45:52.173165946Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hsvho","depends_on_id":"bd-12o4t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:46:38.474072232Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hsvho","depends_on_id":"bd-17w24","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:46:46.019274640Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hsvho","depends_on_id":"bd-1ri6i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:46:31.263646640Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hsvho","depends_on_id":"bd-2fp5r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:46:34.500132925Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hsvho","depends_on_id":"bd-2oovu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:00:06.631662795Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hsvho","depends_on_id":"bd-315zw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:00:06.356645053Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hsvho","depends_on_id":"bd-33fdz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:51:11.331150446Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hsvho","depends_on_id":"bd-pt0dv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:46:28.246583521Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hsvho","depends_on_id":"bd-u4fgt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:46:41.599219605Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-hudcn","title":"Mermaid Terminal Diagram Engine (TME)","description":"Goal\n- Build a world-class Mermaid-to-terminal diagram engine that produces beautiful, deterministic Unicode diagrams with ASCII fallback, adaptive to screen size and terminal constraints.\n\nWhy\n- Current Mermaid support is raw-code display; we need real rendering to showcase FrankenTUI's rendering kernel and layout capabilities.\n- Terminal constraints make layout beauty non-trivial; this epic formalizes the pipeline, algorithms, and integration.\n\nNon-Goals\n- No graphical raster output; terminal-only.\n- No non-deterministic heuristics; all choices must be reproducible from inputs.\n- No executing Mermaid JS; only safe, deterministic parsing.\n\nSuccess Criteria\n- Mermaid fenced blocks render as diagrams, not raw code.\n- Output is deterministic for given input + size + config + capability profile.\n- Diagrams scale: dense and detailed on large screens; readable on small screens.\n- Unicode-first with ASCII fallback; capability policy chooses safely.\n- Styling directives (classDef/style/linkStyle) and init directives are honored as far as terminal allows.\n- Comprehensive tests (unit, property, snapshots, PTY E2E) + JSONL logs covering warnings, guard triggers, and config snapshots.\n\nKey Design Principles\n- Determinism, explainability, and layout beauty over strict spec fidelity.\n- Unicode-first with ASCII fallback and width-calibrated glyph policy.\n- Explicit layout objectives and constraints; no hidden heuristics.\n- Graceful degradation under complexity guards; never crash on oversized  {"id":"bd-hudcn.1","title":"Mermaid Full Coverage Program: eliminate unsupported types + terminal-optimal parity","description":"Background
- Current demo evidence shows gitGraph, journey, and requirementDiagram rendering as unsupported.
- This is a product-level correctness gap: unsupported badges in the flagship showcase undermine the core claim that FrankenTUI is a serious deterministic terminal rendering engine.
- The existing Mermaid epic (bd-hudcn) established core engine primitives and several diagram types, but coverage is still incomplete relative to modern Mermaid families.

Goal
- Deliver COMPLETE, production-grade, deterministic terminal support for all Mermaid diagram families in scope for our pinned Mermaid baseline, with no unsupported placeholders in the demo for those families.

Scope
- Finish urgent missing types: gitGraph, journey, requirementDiagram.
- Add remaining modern families not yet tracked as dedicated beads (timeline/quadrant/xy/sankey/C4/block/packet/architecture).
- Extend fixtures, snapshots, PTY E2E, compatibility matrix, and CI gates so regressions are caught automatically.

Non-negotiables
- Deterministic output for fixed input+size+config+cap profile.
- Unicode-first with robust ASCII fallback policy.
- No silent degradation: warnings/guards must be explicit and testable.

Definition of Done
- Demo showcase no longer shows unsupported badges for canonical supported families.
- Canonical type-coverage matrix is explicit, version-pinned, and enforced by tests.
- Snapshot + E2E suites cover every family in this program.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:47:51.006079057Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T19:55:37.217534650Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":903,"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Planning rationale:\n1) We separate governance (registry/audit/policy/fixtures/gates) from implementation so completeness is measurable, not subjective.\n2) We model unsupported-state elimination as a first-class deliverable (bd-hudcn.1.5), not an incidental side effect.\n3) CI completion gate (bd-hudcn.1.6) is intentionally downstream of policy+fixtures+demo parity so failures are actionable and not noisy.\n4) Existing in-progress type tasks (sequence/class/gantt/mindmap) are explicitly wired into demo parity to avoid shipping partial family coverage while claiming completion.\n\nTerminal-specific quality bar:\n- readability first at 80x24, richer detail at 120x40+, deterministic in both regimes.\n- fallback/degradation must preserve semantics and emit stable warning codes.\n- all family implementations must be evidence-friendly (hashable IR/render outputs + structured PTY logs).","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:52:02Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-hudcn.1.1","title":"Canonical Mermaid family registry + version pin for coverage gating","description":"Background
- We cannot claim complete Mermaid support without an explicit source-of-truth list of diagram families.
- Mermaid continues to evolve; unsupported states often occur when parser dispatch and demo fixture inventories drift.

Goal
- Establish a version-pinned canonical Mermaid diagram-family registry for this repo, including status fields: supported, partial, unsupported, and intentionally out-of-scope.

Scope
- Enumerate canonical families for our chosen Mermaid baseline (core + beta families we choose to support).
- Record per-family acceptance semantics, minimum feature slices, and terminal-specific degradations allowed.
- Add machine-readable mapping used by tests and demo picker assertions.

Deliverables
- Canonical registry artifact integrated into ftui-extras/mermaid pipeline.
- Per-family checklist (parser, IR, layout, render, fixtures, snapshots, PTY E2E, demo picker).

Acceptance Criteria
- Every family in the baseline appears exactly once in the registry.
- CI/tests can consume this registry to detect unsupported regressions.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"EmeraldOwl","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:48:25.940812217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T20:08:37.301659981Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","governance","mermaid"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:48:25.940812217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":915,"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution note: claimed as first active task from Mermaid full-coverage program. Immediate output will be a machine-readable family registry (version-pinned), then downstream audit/fixtures/policy tasks will consume this registry as single source-of-truth for coverage gates and demo parity assertions.","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:55:52Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-hudcn.1.10","title":"Diagram Type: timeline (full terminal-native support)","description":"Goal

 {"id":"bd-hudcn.1.11","title":"Diagram Type: quadrantChart (full terminal-native support)","description":"Goal
- Add full quadrantChart support with accurate quadrant boundaries, axes, and point labeling.

- Render axis grid and point markers with collision-aware labels.
- Provide deterministic fallback behavior when point density is high.

Acceptance Criteria
- Quadrant charts render legibly in 80x24/120x40 and are deterministic.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:50:10.781053135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T19:52:13.557800753Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagram-type","mermaid","quadrant"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.11","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:50:10.781053135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.11","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:26.973460719Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.11","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:27.265714401Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":906,"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.11","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Inclusion rationale: quadrantChart is a valid Mermaid family used for prioritization/tradeoff communication.  {"id":"bd-hudcn.1.6","title":"CI completion gate: zero unsupported canonical Mermaid families","description":"Background
- We need a hard gate to prevent regression back to unsupported states after implementation lands.

Goal
- Add CI-level assertions that enforce zero unsupported canonical families and complete test coverage wiring for all families in the program.

Scope
- Add/extend automated checks that fail when:
  1) a canonical supported family resolves to unsupported in renderer/demo,
  2) a canonical family is missing from fixtures/snapshots/e2e manifests,
  3) warning/fallback codes diverge from policy.
- Integrate with existing snapshot and PTY validation jobs.

Deliverables
- Reproducible gate command(s) and CI wiring.
- Clear failure output keyed by family ID and fixture.

Acceptance Criteria
- A missing family implementation or regression fails CI deterministically.
- Gate references canonical registry from bd-hudcn.1.1 as single source of truth.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:48:59.922204215Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T19:52:35.718411761Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","mermaid","quality-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1yzfb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:46.249752798Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2c2jj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:45.956941021Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:48:59.922204215Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:44.779144406Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:45.076582091Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:45.368183051Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:45.657291926Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":914,"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution note: CI gate is deliberately downstream of parity + policy + fixtures + existing snapshot/e2e tasks. The intent is to make gate failures actionable by family and fixture, not generic 'coverage incomplete' noise.","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:52:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-hudcn.1.7","title":"Diagram Type: gitGraph (full terminal-native support)","description":"Background
- gitGraph is currently surfaced as unsupported in the demo and is a high-signal diagram family for developers.

Goal
- Implement full terminal-native gitGraph support (parser->IR->layout->render) with deterministic commit lane behavior and merge semantics.

Scope
- Support core gitGraph statements: commit/branch/checkout/merge/cherry-pick and styling/state annotations supported in our baseline.
- Produce clear lane assignment, branch divergence/convergence rendering, and readable commit labels under constrained widths.
- Ensure deterministic lane reuse and tie-break rules.

Acceptance Criteria
- gitGraph renders as first-class diagram in demo and markdown fences.
- Snapshot + PTY E2E evidence shows stable topology and labels.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:49:06.331136662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T19:52:02.673597758Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagram-type","gitgraph","mermaid"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:49:06.331136662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:24.541134022Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:24.850729406Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":902,"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"gitGraph design intent:\n- prioritize topological correctness over decorative fidelity.\n- lane assignment must be deterministic with explicit tie-break rules (no heuristic nondeterminism).\n- merge visualization should remain understandable in narrow terminals; ASCII fallback must preserve branch ancestry semantics.\n- commit labels should degrade gracefully (truncate/ellipsis) while keeping branch/head references visible where possible.","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:52:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-hudcn.1.7.1","title":"gitGraph parser + IR normalization","description":"Goal
- Add robust gitGraph parsing and IR normalization with deterministic semantics.

Scope
- Extend tokenizer/parser for gitGraph directives and statement variants.
- Normalize into explicit commit DAG + lane events + branch-head pointers.
- Define deterministic tie-breaks for ambiguous branch/lane placement.
- Emit actionable diagnostics for malformed sequences.

Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests cover representative branch/merge/cherry-pick flows and malformed input diagnostics.
- IR is stable and hashable for evidence logging.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldBear","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:49:12.825459379Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T20:16:40.793345329Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T20:16:40.793322887Z","close_reason":"gitGraph parser + IR normalization implemented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gitgraph","ir","mermaid","parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:49:12.825459379Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-hudcn.1.7.2","title":"gitGraph layout + renderer (lanes, merges, labels, ASCII fallback)","description":"Goal
- Render gitGraph commit DAGs with lane clarity and minimal visual ambiguity in terminal grids.

Scope
- Implement lane assignment, branch line routing, merge visualization, and commit node glyph strategy.
- Optimize label placement and truncation so commit refs remain readable at 80x24.
- Define ASCII fallback mapping for branch/merge glyphs.

Acceptance Criteria
- Merges and branch topology remain legible across small and large terminal sizes.

- Optimize label placement and truncation so commit refs remain readable at 80x24.
- Define ASCII fallback mapping for branch/merge glyphs.

Acceptance Criteria
- Merges and branch topology remain legible across small and large terminal sizes.

 {"id":"bd-hudcn.1.7.3","title":"gitGraph validation + snapshots/e2e + demo integration","description":"Goal
- Complete gitGraph production hardening with fixtures, snapshots, PTY E2E, and demo integration.

Scope
- Add fixture set covering linear history, diverge/merge, rebases/cherry-picks, and failure diagnostics.
- Add snapshot coverage at 80x24 and 120x40 plus ASCII fallback.
- Add PTY E2E logs for gitGraph family and wire into Mermaid showcase picker.

Acceptance Criteria
- No unsupported gitGraph state in demo for canonical fixtures.
- Snapshot and E2E checks are deterministic and stable.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:49:23.773869641Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:33.453587533Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","gitgraph","mermaid","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:33.453531588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:33.141219299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:49:23.773869641Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:31.344916478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-hudcn.1.8","title":"Diagram Type: journey (full terminal-native support)","description":"Background
- journey diagrams are currently unsupported in demo output and are important for UX/story mapping use cases.

Goal
- Implement full journey diagram support with stage/section semantics and score visualization optimized for terminals.

Scope
- Parse journeys (sections, tasks, actors, scores/status markers).
- Render clear row/column structure with progress/intensity cues and compact legends.
- Support deterministic wrapping/truncation for long step labels.

Acceptance Criteria
- journey family renders as first-class in demo and markdown fences.
- Terminal output remains readable at 80x24 and scales gracefully.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:49:30.561417438Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T19:52:02.721769199Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagram-type","journey","mermaid"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.8","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:49:30.561417438Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.8","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:25.150481022Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.8","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:51:25.452834941Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":904,"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"journey design intent:\n- terminal output should make stage transitions and actor responsibilities immediately scannable.\n- score/status encoding must work in both color and low-capability/ascii fallback modes.\n- long task text is expected; wrapping/truncation policy should preserve the strongest semantic tokens first (stage/task identifiers).","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:52:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-hudcn.1.8.1","title":"journey parser + IR normalization","description":"Goal
- Add journey parser and IR normalization with explicit section/task/actor semantics.

Scope
- Parse section boundaries, task entries, actor participation, and score/status tokens.
- Normalize IR for deterministic row ordering and actor column assignment.
- Emit diagnostics for malformed scoring and missing section context.

Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests cover representative valid/invalid journey inputs.
- IR stable for hashing and downstream render stages.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:49:41.743132678Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T20:16:41.106119652Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T20:16:41.106097901Z","close_reason":"journey parser + IR normalization implemented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ir","journey","mermaid","parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hudcn.1.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn.1.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T19:49:41.743132678Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
